user.title: Set up LLM Watch
user.description:
One-time hub setup: pick a search backend and set your default AI. Add individual watches afterwards from the integration page.

Backend setup:
- **Tavily** (recommended): create a free account at tavily.com, copy the API key (tvly-...). 1,000 free searches a month, and it extracts page content for you, which avoids retailer bot-blocking.
- **SearXNG** (fully local): your own instance URL. Enable the JSON format in settings.yml under search: formats: [html, json].
- **Brave Search**: get an API key at brave.com/search/api (free tier available). Results only; pages are fetched separately.

data_description.backend: Only used by web search watches; page watches work regardless.
data_description.ai_task_entity: Used by watches that don't pick their own. Leave empty to use the preferred AI Task entity from Settings > Voice assistants.

data_description.backend: Only used by web search watches; page watches work regardless.
data_description.ai_task_entity: Used by watches that don't pick their own. Leave empty to use the preferred AI Task entity from Settings > Voice assistants.

abort.already_configured: LLM Watch is already set up. Add watches from the integration page.

error.missing_tavily_api_key: Tavily is selected but no API key was entered.
error.missing_searxng_url: SearXNG is selected but no URL was entered.
error.missing_brave_api_key: Brave Search is selected but no API key was entered.

user.title: Add a page watch
user.description: Watches one URL. Describe what you want in plain English; the watch fetches the page on a schedule and turns on when it finds it.

data.name: Watch name
data.require_in_stock: Only count items that are in stock
data.create_anyway: Skip the test run and create anyway

data_description.url: For JavaScript-heavy sites, use the site's JSON API endpoint instead (find it in your browser's DevTools Network tab).
data_description.prompt: For example: an air conditioning unit, portable or fixed.

preview.title: Test run result

data.name: Watch name
data.require_in_stock: Only count items that are in stock

error.cannot_connect: Could not fetch the page. Check the URL.
error.no_content: The page produced almost no text. It is probably rendered with JavaScript; point the watch at the site's JSON API instead, or tick 'create anyway'.
error.ai_task_error: The AI task failed. Check that an AI Task entity exists (add the AI Task sub-entry to your AI integration), and see the log for details.
error.bad_model_reply: The model did not return usable data. Try a different model, or tick 'create anyway'.

user.title: Add a web search watch
user.description: No URL needed. On each run the AI writes search queries, your search backend finds candidate pages, and the AI checks each one against your description. The test run can take a few minutes on a local model.

data.name: Watch name
data.require_in_stock: Only count items that are in stock
data.create_anyway: Skip the test run and create anyway

data_description.prompt: For example: a portable air conditioning unit, at least 9000 BTU, from a UK retailer.
data_description.sites: Optional, comma separated (e.g. screwfix.com, argos.co.uk). Best for stock checks at specific retailers.

preview.title: Test run result

data.name: Watch name
data.require_in_stock: Only count items that are in stock

error.cannot_connect: Could not reach the search backend or fetch the result pages.
error.no_content: The search returned nothing readable. Check the hub's backend settings, or broaden the description.
error.ai_task_error: The AI task failed. Check that an AI Task entity exists, and see the log for details.
error.bad_model_reply: The model did not return usable data. Try a different model.
error.no_backend: The hub's search backend isn't configured. Reconfigure the LLM Watch integration first (pick a backend and enter its URL or API key).
